affordances.name: Mode separated option cycle
affordances.mechanism: When a problem needs both creative option breadth and disciplined narrowing, require the answer to separate the divergent phase from the convergent phase, name the switching point, and avoid chaotic mode-mixing that prevents either ideation or evaluation from working.

use_when: The team is prematurely collapsing to the first workable idea or endlessly ideating without a selection moment.
The problem is novel enough to need broad option generation but consequential enough to require disciplined narrowing.
Creative exploration and critical evaluation are both necessary but destructive when mixed at the same time.
do_not_use_when: The team switches modes so chaotically that neither ideation nor evaluation gets done well.
The divergent phase is being cut short by early judgment or anchoring.
Convergent pressure is being used to force an obvious answer before the option space is mapped.
case_evidence_needed: Whether the current failure is premature convergence, endless divergence, or chaotic mode-switching.
The options or problem frames that still need widening.
The selection criteria or evidence that will narrow the options.
The explicit moment when the team should switch from could to should.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to name whether the case needs divergence, convergence, or a staged cycle, then define how the widened option set will be narrowed without collapsing too early.
evidence_required: The option space that needs widening.
good_output_shape: Names the stage the case is in.
Defines how and when selection begins.
Avoids evaluating ideas while generating them.

diagnostic_questions: Are we stuck because we narrowed too early, widened too long, or mixed both modes badly?
What could we do before we ask what we should do?
What evidence or criterion marks the switch from divergence to convergence?
misuse_guards: Do not call chaotic switching balanced thinking.
Do not evaluate options during the generation phase.
Do not use convergence to avoid mapping the real option space.

source_evidence.source_quote: Most useful when** the team is either prematurely collapsing to the first workable idea or endlessly ideating without a selection moment.
creative exploration and critical evaluation are both necessary, but destructive when mixed at the same time.
asking **"What *could* you do?"** promotes the expansive exploration of divergent thinking

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 11 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports a staged divergence/convergence handoff rather than generic creativity advice.
dropped_material: Did not extract design thinking, scenario analysis, or one-day answer as separate affordances.
Kept focused/diffuse mode material as context for stage separation.
open_questions: PR49 should test whether this card helps a packet know whether to widen or narrow without creating mode-management clutter.

absence_records.reason: The source warns that participants can think they are balanced while switching modes so chaotically that neither ideation nor evaluation gets done well.

source_evidence.source_quote: participants think they are being “balanced” but are actually switching modes so chaotically that neither ideation nor evaluation gets done well.

absence_records.reason: The source says early evaluation and anchoring can cut short divergence and hamper originality, so PR48 must not collapse options too soon.

source_evidence.source_quote: Cutting short the divergent phase by evaluating ideas too quickly **"run[s] the risk of hampering originality"**.

absence_records.reason: The source distinguishes real divergence from high-volume, low-variety idea generation; PR85 should not promote many same-shaped ideas as genuine option widening.

source_evidence.source_quote: Generating high volume, but low variety—ideas that are merely variations on a theme.
divergence and convergence are the **"engines of creative problem solving"**.

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 11 source reading found strong reviewed depth for divergent-vs-convergent-thinking as a mode-separated option cycle. PR85 adds a narrow absence rail for superficial divergence, but no new positive affordance.
dropped_material: PR85 rejected superficial divergence as a positive split because volume without variety only hardens the existing divergence-quality guard.
No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, deterministic option selection, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether this card improves widen/narrow timing while distinguishing real option variety from many same-shaped ideas.
